ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "epoch" INTEGER /* Epoch time when the tweet was created */,
  "weekday" TEXT /* Day of the week when the tweet was created */,
  "hour" INTEGER /* Hour of the day when the tweet was created */,
  "month" TEXT /* Month when the tweet was created */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the month when the tweet was created */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the tweet was created */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the tweet location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the tweet location */,
  "text" TEXT /* Content of the tweet */,
  "lang" TEXT /* Language of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of quotes */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "original_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the original tweet if it is a retweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is verified */,
  "user_followers_count" INTEGER /* Count of followers the user has */,
  "user_statuses_count" INTEGER /* Count of statuses (tweets) posted by the user */,
  "user_description" TEXT /* Description or bio of the user */,
  "user_friends_count" INTEGER /* Count of friends (followings) of the user */,
  "user_location" TEXT /* Location information of the user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "user_lang" TEXT /* Preferred language of the user */,
  "user_screen_name" TEXT /* Screen name (username) of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user account was created */,
  "profile_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile image */,
  "last_seen" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was last active */,
  "is_bot" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is a bot */,
  "account_type" TEXT /* Type of user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hashtags" (
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hashtag */,
  "hashtag_text" TEXT /* Text of the hashtag */,
  "trend_score" INTEGER /* Popularity or trend score of the hashtag */,
  "first_used_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the hashtag was first used */,
  "last_used_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the hashtag was last used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_mentions" (
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mention record */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that contains the mention */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user that is mentioned */,
  "mention_text" TEXT /* Text of the mention */,
  "mention_context" TEXT /* Context of the mention (e.g., reply, retweet, quote) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mention_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_mentions_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets_hashtags" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that uses the hashtag */,
  "hashtag_id" INTEGER /* ID of the hashtag used in the tweet */,
  "usage_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the hashtag is used in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "hashtag_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_followers" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being followed */,
  "follower_id" INTEGER /* ID of the follower */,
  "followed_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the follower relationship was established */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "follower_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_statuses" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet posted by the user */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is currently active */,
  "last_status_change" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user's status was last changed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets_geo" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location related to the tweet */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location related to the tweet */,
  "city" TEXT /* City name of the tweet's location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country name of the tweet's location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the tweet's location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets_user_mentions" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that contains the mention */,
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mention record */,
  "mention_order" INTEGER /* Order of mentions in the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id", "mention_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "languages" (
  "lang_id" INTEGER /* ID for the language */,
  "lang_code" TEXT /* Language code (e.g., 'en' for English) */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Full name of the language */,
  "language_family" TEXT /* Language family */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lang_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_privacy_settings" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "geo_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user has geo-enabled settings */,
  "protected" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's account is protected */,
  "dm_enabled" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user allows direct messages */,
  "profile_public" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user's profile is public */,
  "privacy_policy" TEXT /* Privacy policy settings of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the tweet */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the access request */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
